name: backend-models
description: Define database models and ORM entities with proper naming, relationships, validation, and data integrity constraints. Use this skill when creating or modifying model classes, database table definitions, model relationships (one-to-many, many-to-many), data validation rules, database constraints, or model methods. Apply when working with ORM model files, ActiveRecord, SQLAlchemy, Sequelize, Prisma schemas, or any database model definitions that map objects to database tables and enforce data structure and relationships.
capabilities: Define database models with proper naming conventions
Add validation rules and database constraints
Set up foreign keys and cascade behaviors
author: EIS-ITS
securitySummary: All 21 static findings are false positives. The skill is pure documentation containing no executable code, network calls, or file operations. Findings misclassify metadata URLs as hardcoded network targets, relative documentation paths as path traversal, and descriptive text fields as cryptographic code. The pre-existing security audit in skill-report.json correctly assessed this as safe.
valueStatement: Database models often lack proper validation and relationships. This skill provides clear guidance for defining database models with proper naming conventions, relationships, constraints, and data integrity rules.
userTitle: Design Database Models and ORM Entities
limitations: Does not generate actual code files
Does not connect to databases or run migrations
Only provides guidance, not automated implementation

useCases.title: Create ORM Entities
useCases.description: Define database models using SQLAlchemy, Sequelize, Prisma, or ActiveRecord with proper relationships and validation.

useCases.title: Design Data Schema
useCases.description: Set up database tables with foreign keys, indexes, constraints, and data types for web applications.

useCases.title: Enforce Data Integrity
useCases.description: Implement cascade behaviors, unique constraints, and validation rules to maintain data quality.

promptTemplates.title: Create User Model
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a user model with email, password_hash, and name fields. Add email uniqueness validation and required password field.

promptTemplates.title: Define Post Relationship
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a post model that belongs to a user. Set up the relationship on both sides with proper foreign keys.

promptTemplates.title: Many-to-Many Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up a many-to-many relationship between posts and tags using a join table with cascade delete.

promptTemplates.title: Add Model Scopes
promptTemplates.prompt: Add scopes to the article model for published articles and articles by specific authors.

bestPractices: Use explicit foreign key names that indicate both the table and column
Add database-level constraints alongside application validation
Set up indexes on fields frequently used in WHERE clauses and JOINs
antiPatterns: Using vague field names like 'data' or 'info' instead of specific names
Skipping foreign key constraints for convenience
Omitting created_at and updated_at timestamps on audit-relevant models

faq.question: Which ORMs does this skill support?
faq.answer: SQLAlchemy, Sequelize, Prisma, ActiveRecord, and other major ORM frameworks.

faq.question: Does this skill create actual database tables?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance for writing model code. You run migrations separately.

faq.question: Can it help with complex many-to-many relationships?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides guidance on setting up join tables with proper cascade behaviors.

faq.question: Does it validate existing database connections?
faq.answer: No. The skill only provides coding guidance, not runtime database operations.

faq.question: Can it help with migration file generation?
faq.answer: It can guide you on writing model definitions. Actual migration tools are separate.

faq.question: Does it work with both SQL and NoSQL databases?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers relational models and can guide NoSQL document structures.
